summary.intent: Complete a multi-round Codex code review cycle for PR #675 on fogodev/ars-ui, addressing reviewer findings on CheckboxGroup and RadioGroup components through iterative fix-validate-push-resolve loops.
summary.outcome: Two full Codex review passes were completed: first pass findings were resolved and pushed as commit aee6a085, and second pass findings (3 new threads) were fixed, validated with cargo test/clippy/llvm-cov/insta, and committed as a follow-up addressing checkbox max clamp, radio focus/label, and ItemLabel `for` attribute target.

repo: The repo uses a Codex bot review cycle tracked via GitHub reactions (👀 = in-progress, +1 = approved) and GraphQL review thread queries to automate the review polling loop.
Nightly rustfmt is enforced separately from stable; cargo +nightly fmt --all --check is a required gate before commit.
cargo insta is used for snapshot testing with --unreferenced=reject as a strict gate; snapshot files must be explicitly accepted (mv *.snap.new *.snap) when intentional changes occur.
cargo llvm-cov is used for coverage gating on state-machine logic changes in ars-components.
Spec files in spec/components/, spec/leptos-components/, and spec/dioxus-components/ must be kept in sync with implementation changes.
Commits addressing Codex review include a Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com> trailer and a @codex review trigger comment to start the next pass.

code.finding: Uncontrolled CheckboxGroup did not clamp checked items when max_checked prop shrinks; fix required clamping logic on props update.
Re-registering a focused item as disabled did not clear focus or state; needed explicit focus/state reset in that code path.
ItemLabel `for` attribute was pointing at the role=div container instead of the hidden radio input; corrected target to the input element ID.
Snapshot for radio_group_item_label changed from 'shipping-item-standard' to 'shipping-item-standard-input' as a direct result of the ItemLabel for-attribute fix; intentional update.

workflow: The review loop polls GitHub reactions and unresolved thread count in a bash while-loop with 120s sleep intervals to detect Codex review state changes.
Review threads are resolved via GraphQL after posting fix summaries as replies, then a @codex review comment re-triggers the next pass.
cargo target locks can cause conflicts when overlapping cargo jobs; running clippy to completion before starting test runs avoids lock contention.
Formatter (nightly fmt) should be run after apply_patch operations because patch application may not match nightly formatting style.

friction: Nightly rustfmt found a wrapping issue in new checkbox code after patches were applied, requiring an extra fmt+check cycle before commit.
The Codex polling loop requires manual intervention when unresolved threads appear mid-loop (exit code 2 path), breaking automation.
cargo clippy ran slowly through all test targets in ars-components, blocking the pipeline while waiting for target locks.
open_items: Third Codex pass not yet triggered/completed at transcript end — the second-pass commit was pushed but @codex review re-trigger and thread resolution for pass 2 are not shown as completed.
The attr_passthrough.rs file in ars-dioxus was checked for formatter drift (suggesting it caused trouble earlier) but the root cause of that prior issue was not documented.
Coverage numbers from llvm-cov runs were not captured/thresholded — only pass/fail was checked, not whether coverage regressed.
